title: Move hardcoded production secrets into environment variables
description:
# Issue #250 - Production credentials committed to the repository

starter/settings.py contains the production database password and a live API key, and DEBUG defaults to True in production. Rotate-and-remove is already underway; your job is the code fix.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- DATABASE_URL, API_KEY and DEBUG are read from environment variables (os.environ)
- no secret literal remains in the source (no password, no sk-live key)
- DEBUG defaults to False when unset and parses common truthy strings ('1', 'true', 'yes')
- safe defaults only: the fallback DATABASE_URL must not embed credentials
optimal_hint: Ideal: os.environ.get('DATABASE_URL', 'postgresql://localhost:5432/shop'), os.environ.get('API_KEY', ''), DEBUG parsed via os.environ.get('DEBUG', 'false').lower() in ('1', 'true', 'yes'). The getters must reflect the module-level values; tests reload the module with patched env vars.
